# UC-17.1.27 · Cloudflare WAF and Firewall Events — Blocks and Managed Challenges

## Description

Firewall events capture the first-class action Cloudflare applied to each request—blocks, interactive challenges, and connection closes. Spikes grouped by rule ID and source product (WAF, rate limiting, bot management) reveal attack campaigns and policy regressions.

## Value

Gives security teams a single pane for edge enforcement without scraping multiple Cloudflare dashboards during an incident.

## Implementation

Add a Logpush job for the `firewall_events` dataset with HEC sourcetype `cloudflare:json` (or the app-documented sourcetype). Ensure `Action`, `Kind`, `RuleID`, `Source`, `ClientIP`, and `ClientRequestHost` are included. Create suppressions for trusted scanner IPs. Map `Source=waf` events into MITRE web attack techniques in ES if used.

## SPL

```spl
index=web sourcetype="cloudflare:json" Kind="firewall" earliest=-24h
| eval act=lower(Action)
| where act IN ("block","jschallenge","challenge","managedchallenge","connectionclose")
| stats count by ClientIP, ClientRequestHost, RuleID, Source, Description
| sort -count
| head 40
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar (actions over time), Table (RuleID, Description, volume), Pie chart (Source breakdown).

## References

- [Cloudflare Docs — Firewall events dataset](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/logpush/logpush-job/datasets/zone/firewall_events/)
